content.full_text:
FRANK M. MOORE
ATTORNEY AT LAW
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

4085 LONG BEACH BOULEVARD
LONG BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90807
TEL (213) 427-0921
FAX (213) 426-6912

Dec 6 11 31 AM 9

December 3, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
428 J Street
Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

The undersigned has been consulted by local persons from a
well known political party. It is their desire to open up a
campaign headquarters to further the interest of the political
party.

It is contemplated that the headquarters will be listed in the
telephone book to answer questions from voters, individuals at the
headquarters will do typical campaign chores such as stuffing
envelopes, etc., and the headquarters will be utilized for purposes
of voter registration, etc. At no time is it contemplated that the
headquarters will give any monetary donations to political
candidates or purchase advertising, etc., on behalf of political
candidates.

In a review of this matter, we have had an opportunity to
review the statutes as well as information prepared by the Fair
Political Practices Commission. However, we find nothing that
relates specifically to the above referenced facts. The only items
reviewed so far relate specifically to disclosure of contributions
by specific candidates and disclosure by non controlled political
committees.

We would appreciate your view of these facts and a
determination of what is necessary, if anything, for proper
reporting under the law. We would appreciate your comments in
writing so we might have a written record for purposes of advising
the local persons at this time and in the future.

Fair Political Practices Commission
December 3, 1991
Page Two


Thank you for your help in this matter.

Very truly yours,

LAW OFFICES OF FRANK M. MOORE
A Professional Corporation


By: [signature]
WILLIAM E. SZCZEPANIAK

WES/jgm

sections.question: What reporting requirements, if any, apply to a local political party's campaign headquarters that will engage in voter outreach, voter registration, and typical campaign activities (such as stuffing envelopes) but will not make monetary donations to candidates or purchase advertising on behalf of candidates?
sections.question_synthetic: An attorney seeks FPPC guidance on whether a political party campaign headquarters engaged in voter outreach and registration activities, but not making contributions or purchasing candidate advertising, has any reporting obligations under California political practices law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The question is clearly stated but no CONCLUSION section exists in this document. Document appears complete as submitted.

embedding.summary: An attorney from Long Beach requests FPPC guidance on reporting requirements for a local political party campaign headquarters that will conduct voter outreach, voter registration, and standard campaign activities but will not make monetary contributions to candidates or purchase advertising.
